reason: Both answers commit to A, correctly identify the first-person/verifiable-claim vs. second-person/presumptuous-clickbait dynamic, note that B's "hiding in your config" pattern-matches to growth-hacking content marketing, and both include the same caveat (only works if the post delivers) and nearly identical "optional upgrade" headline suggestion. B is formatted with bold sub-bullets and adds one slightly sharper phrasing ("$340k is your number dressed up as their risk — a subtle dishonesty engineers spot instantly"), but this is essentially the same point A makes about pattern-matching to clickbait, just phrased differently — not a new decision-relevant fact. Substantively the two answers are equivalent in correctness, coverage of key points, and actionable conclusion; the extra length/formatting in B doesn't add anything that would change what the asker does next.
